text.title: Architecture Workflows Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when workflow ceremony detaches from decision substance. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Workflow runs to completion; every step has a checkbox; the actual decision was made offline before the workflow started.

root-cause.source: Internal — workflow audit on 12 architecture activities 2025

antipattern.root-cause: Workflow used as a rubber stamp; review gates skipped or self-approved.
antipattern.fix: Enforce review_gate=true + named reviewer for any type-1 decision step; validator rejects self-review. See rule review-gate-on-decision.

antipattern.symptom: Steps mark themselves complete without producing the artefact file; downstream review has nothing to verify.
antipattern.root-cause: No materialised artefact requirement; in-memory state lost between subagents.
antipattern.fix: Block step transitions when artefact_path does not exist on disk; validator scans for file presence. See rule materialised-artefact.

antipattern.symptom: One person owns clarifier + designer + critic + documenter; review is self-critique; decisions reflect single perspective.
antipattern.root-cause: Stakeholder map flat; roles collapsed to save coordination cost.
antipattern.fix: Require ≥2 distinct names across roles for any workflow with ≥4 steps; validator counts unique names. See rule role-per-step.

antipattern.symptom: Workflow stalls at step 3 for 6 weeks; nobody cancels because there is no defined abort condition.
antipattern.fix: Block instantiation without abort_condition string; require periodic abort-condition check at every review gate. See rule abort-condition.
